The movie is a mess, but it's an entertaining mess and a good looking mess.  The cast includes Portia Doubleday from "Mr Robot" and Michael Pena as Mr. Rourke.   The plot is more "Knives Out" than "Twilight Zone".   The island has properties that allow people to change things in their past,  but of course the results aren't what they expected.  Not everybody makes it out alive but the survivors learn an important lesson not to waste your life regretting the past.

How about another movie guys, yes the same story do you think more than one story is allowed to get told in Hollywood? How do we get people to know the exactly what is going to happen in our movie? I know! We take all the super important parts of our story and put it in the trailer! I know how we'll get em too, we take all the clips, and put them out of order, i like money, how about another movie guys"

An interesting bit of history. Fantasy Island began on tv in 1977; however, in 1972 a tv movie called "Haunts of the Very Rich" was made with a similar theme. The story is about a bunch of people who are taken to a remote area they originally believe is paradise and discover is closer to hell. In both versions the island is run by a distinguished man wearing all white who claims to be in charge. You can watch it on YouTube and judge for yourself; but, I remember when they came out and thought so at the time.

I have conflicted feelings about this. 

While I appreciate the twist, I'm a little disappointed this couldn't be a more... Magical feel-good film where the characters grow from the experience in a positive way. 

This is clearly not tailored to an older audience and that makes me equally sad. My parents and grandparents wouldn't be very interested in watching this and that is what really disappoints me. 

But in my mind, the idea of defeating a perversion of something sacred has some emotional weight to it. So my Hope is it's not an "escape" film and more of a "let's fix this" thing.
